tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: A Four-Year-Old's Daily Wave Transforms Lonely Hearts Into a Tight-Knit Community
stories.summary: Four-year-old Roman's daily wave transformed his North Carolina neighborhood when neighbor Wade Fulgum stopped by, sparking a dozen families to become close friends and healing Roman's loneliness after his parents' separation.

stories.headline: White Storks Return to Derbyshire After 600 Years—A Living Bridge to the Past
stories.summary: White storks will nest in Derbyshire for the first time since the 14th century in a £300,000 predator-proof enclosure at Willington Wetlands, restoring a missing piece of England's wetland ecosystem.

stories.headline: From Crutches to World Cup Glory: Mikel Merino's Impossible Comeback
stories.summary: Mikel Merino became the first player in FIFA World Cup history to score winning goals as a substitute in two knockout matches—against Portugal in the 91st minute and Belgium in the 87th minute.

stories.headline: College Student's Viral Post Saves Grandmother's Struggling Salvadoran Restaurant
stories.summary: Sophia's Instagram video brought a wave of grateful customers to her grandmother's struggling Maryland restaurant, filling it with community support and hope.

stories.headline: Wicklow's Underdog Rally: 12-Point Deficit Becomes 15-Point Triumph
stories.summary: Wicklow staged a breathtaking second-half comeback from 12 points down at halftime to claim the Tailteann Cup and secure a golden ticket to the 2027 All-Ireland Championship.

stories.headline: Humanoid Robots Perform Surgery for the First Time—Opening Doors to Remote Care
stories.summary: Surgeons at UC San Diego guided two humanoid robots to complete live surgical procedures for the first time, including Dr. Ryan Broderick overseeing a gallbladder removal, proving this technology could bring expert care to remote places.

stories.headline: Strangers and Students Unite to Free an Elderly Driver's Van From the Mud
stories.summary: Twenty-seven-year-old entrepreneur Kai Emilio rallied strangers and students at a Yishun pasar malam to coordinate their push and free an elderly driver's van from the mud, restoring faith in community kindness.

stories.headline: Beaver Community Rallies With Food, Clothes, and Love for Fire-Displaced Families
stories.summary: The town of Beaver rallied with donated essentials and goodie bags for families displaced by the Cottonwood Fire and firefighters battling the blaze across more than 97,000 acres.

stories.headline: Linda Noskova Claims First Grand Slam After Spurning Five Match Points
stories.summary: Twenty-one-year-old Linda Noskova rallied past fellow Czech Karolina Muchova in a thrilling all-Czech Wimbledon final, capturing her breakthrough Grand Slam title.

stories.headline: From 8 Points Down: Merab Dvalishvili's Stunning Comeback Against Olympic Champion
stories.summary: Merab Dvalishvili rallied from an 8-0 deficit to defeat Olympic wrestling champion Henry Cejudo 11-8 in a wild comeback victory at RAF Georgia.

stories.headline: China Catches a Rocket From the Sky—A Global First in Reusable Space Technology
stories.summary: China's Long March-10B rocket was caught mid-air by a net on the Navigator recovery platform in a global first—the first stage descending vertically about six minutes after liftoff with "dead center" precision.

stories.headline: Baby Snail Kites Thrive in the Heart of a Florida Development—A Thrilling Win for Rare Birds
stories.summary: Three baby snail kites were born to separate mating pairs nesting in sable palm trees in a Port St. Lucie wetland preserve, delighting conservationist Debbie Falzarano and proving coexistence with development is possible.

stories.headline: 3,500 Endangered Boreal Toad Tadpoles Released Into Their Historic Breeding Grounds
stories.summary: Volunteers and students released over 3,500 boreal toad tadpoles into historic breeding sites near Leadville in the largest translocation effort yet to save the endangered species.

stories.headline: The First Full-Scale 3D-Printed Nuclear Reactor Powers a Clean Energy Future
stories.summary: A Florida startup unveiled a groundbreaking 3D-printed nuclear module that could power remote areas and data centers reliably for 30 years without refueling.
